chunk_summary: Outpatient energy prescription for growth faltering involves diagnosing energy gaps, setting practical targets, densifying food, and monitoring progress to ensure catch-up growth in children with wasting or faltering weight percentiles.
learning_objectives: Understand the diagnostic steps for identifying energy gaps in children with growth faltering.
Learn practical strategies for food-based energy densification to support catch-up growth.
Recognize clinical indicators for urgent inpatient referral in severe cases of malnutrition.
Develop monitoring protocols to assess progress in outpatient nutritional interventions.
key_facts: Energy targets for catch-up growth often require 10–20% more than age/sex requirements (ICMR-NIN, 2020).
MUAC and clinical risk factors like diarrhea or poor appetite guide urgency of intervention.
Food densification strategies include adding 1–2 teaspoons of oil/ghee, groundnut paste, or protein-rich foods like egg, paneer, or dal.
Recheck weight in 2–4 weeks; upward crossing of percentiles or flattening of weight loss is expected.
Severe wasting (weight-for-length/height < -3 SD) or edema necessitates urgent inpatient referral.
reference_quote: Energy targets for catch-up growth often require 10–20% more than age/sex requirements (ICMR-NIN, 2020).
document_key_topics: Energy gap diagnosis in growth faltering
Monitoring and follow-up for catch-up growth
Criteria for inpatient referral in severe malnutrition
